summary: Closed, unmerged WIP PR that added an initial C decompilation pass for several early Ness fighter routines in ftness.c, plus provisional fighter/item headers and struct offsets needed to express the code. The PR body says this was Chris's work so far and that the branch was being set up as WIP. Assembly globals in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftness.s were annotated as decompiled for func_801147C0, func_8011493C, func_8011495C, func_80114A48, func_80114A90, and func_80114AD8, while the new C file also introduced func_80114B6C. There was no review discussion in the slice, so lessons are limited to the diff evidence.
what_changed: Added new source file src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c with C implementations for ftNess_Reset, func_8011493C, func_8011495C, func_80114A48, func_80114A90, func_80114AD8, and func_80114B6C.
Renamed/decompiled func_801147C0 in C as ftNess_Reset, with a comment noting its former symbol name.
ftNess_Reset casts HSD_GObj data to Fighter, calls func_80074A4C(arg0, 0, 0), and clears Fighter fields x2240, x2244, x2248, x222C, and x224C.
func_8011493C was reduced to a wrapper around func_8011B0F8.
func_8011495C reads arg0->unk2D4 as ftAttributes2D4 and returns the float at offset 0x94.
func_80114A48 and func_80114A90 check func_8026B2B4 on Fighter x1974 before calling fighter helper functions func_80070CC4 or func_80070C48 with argument 1.
func_80114AD8 calls func_80070FB4(arg0, 1, -1), then conditionally calls func_80070CC4(arg0, 1) when arg1 is nonzero.
func_80114B6C calls func_800704F0 twice, once for arg1 1 and once for arg1 0, using lbl_804D96B0.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.h with prototypes for the decompiled Ness functions and required includes.
Expanded src/melee/ft/fighter.h from a mostly opaque data array into a provisional Fighter layout with known offsets around 0x2D4, 0x1974, and 0x222C-0x224C.
Added ftAttributes2D4 with padding to offset 0x94 and a float unk94 field.
Added src/melee/it/it.h with a prototype for func_8026B2B4.
Annotated several ftness.s globals with '# decompiled' comments rather than deleting the assembly in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Used explicit padding arrays in Fighter and ftAttributes2D4 to anchor known field offsets while leaving unknown regions opaque, which is a practical early-decomp technique when full struct layouts are not yet known.
Kept uncertain fields named by offset-style placeholders such as x1974, x222C, x2240, x2244, x2248, x224C, unk2D4, and unk94 rather than inventing semantics not proven by the diff.
Introduced a character-specific header ftness.h to collect prototypes for Ness routines and local dependencies.
Preserved traceability by commenting ftNess_Reset as formerly func_801147C0.
Marked corresponding assembly symbols as decompiled, making progress searchable in the asm file even though this was a WIP branch.
decomp_lessons: For early fighter-character decompilation, use offset-accurate provisional structs to unblock matching small routines, but keep names conservative until behavior is understood.
Small wrappers and field accessors, such as a direct call-through to func_8011B0F8 or returning a float from offset 0x94, are useful low-risk footholds for growing a character source file.
When a routine uses item-system helpers from fighter code, introduce the minimal cross-system header/prototype needed, as this PR did with it.h and func_8026B2B4.
Documenting the old assembly symbol next to a newly named C function helps future contributors connect decompiled names to map/asm addresses.
Because this PR was closed unmerged and WIP, future work should verify the provisional Fighter layout and function prototypes against current headers before reuse.
naming_conventions: Character-specific function naming used camel-style prefix ftNess_, shown by ftNess_Reset for former func_801147C0.
Unknown or not-yet-understood functions retained address-derived names such as func_8011493C, func_8011495C, func_80114A48, func_80114A90, func_80114AD8, and func_80114B6C.
Struct fields with known offsets but unknown meaning were named with offset-like or unk-style names: x1974, x222C, x223C, x2240, x2244, x2248, x224C, unk2D4, unk94.
Header guards used lower-case names with surrounding underscores in this WIP slice, such as _ness_h_ and _it_h_.
The new attribute helper struct was named ftAttributes2D4 to reflect the Fighter pointer at offset 0x2D4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Annotated asm/melee/ft/chara/ftness.s global labels with '# decompiled' for the routines covered by the new C source.
Matched field offsets by replacing broad opaque arrays with padding arrays sized to exact offsets, for example data0[0x2D4], data2D8[0x169C], data1978[0x8B8], and ftAttributes2D4 data[0x94].
Represented float constant/data use through lbl_804D96B0 declared in fighter.h and passed to func_800704F0.
Used direct casts from HSD_GObj->data to Fighter* to express common fighter object access patterns.
Used simple conditional structure around func_8026B2B4(...) == 0 to recover branch behavior in func_80114A48 and func_80114A90.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human context in the body says: "This is Chris's work so far on ftness. Setting up a WIP branch for now."
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs or current master for the final merged Ness decompilation of ftNess_Reset and adjacent ftness routines.
Verify whether Fighter offsets x1974 and x222C-x224C later received semantic field names.
Check whether ftAttributes2D4 was replaced by a more accurate attributes struct in later fighter headers.
Search for func_8026B2B4 usage to determine the item-related condition used by func_80114A48 and func_80114A90.
Compare this WIP branch against later ftness.c changes before reusing any prototype or header layout.
